title: Echinolobium humile
intro: Echinolobium humile, also known as the dwarf pala-pala or dwarf palo-palo, is a small, shrub-like plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, contributing to its potential benefits in reducing pain, inflammation, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Echinolobium humile include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Echinolobium humile is commonly used in traditional medicine and is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, as well as being used in capsule or powder form to take advantage of its health benefits.
taxonomy: Echinolobium humile is classified under the family Fabaceae, which is commonly known as the legume family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its botanical features. As a member of the Fabaceae family, Echinolobium humile is a type of flowering plant that produces seeds in pods. The genus Echinolobium is a relatively rare and lesser-known genus, with only a few species recognized within it. Further classification of the plant may involve the family's subfamilies, tribes, or even subtribes, but Echinolobium humile is primarily classified as a member of the Fabaceae family.
benefits: Echinolobium humile, also known as the Yellow Cordia, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its potential health benefits.
